origin.name: Ivory Coast
origin.key_document: Acte de deces (death certificate from Centre d'etat civil, requires certified English translation)
origin.source: FCDO Ivory Coast travel advice, updated 2025; British Embassy Abidjan guidance
origin.cremation_note: Cremation in Ivory Coast is available. Bringing ashes home to the UK is an option alongside full body repatriation.

destinations.name: United Kingdom
key_documents: Acte de deces (certified English translation required)
special_rules: All French-language documentation requires certified English translation for UK acceptance.
Police clearance is required for all violent or suspicious deaths.
An export permit from the relevant Ivorian authority is required for all repatriations.
Embalming must be carried out by a licensed funeral director before air transport.
destinations.dest_reception: The UK funeral director takes custody at the cargo terminal. All documentation must be in certified English translation. The coroner for the district is notified. Straightforward cases proceed directly to funeral arrangements.
destinations.dest_consular_note: FCDO 24-hour emergency line: +44 (0)20 7008 5000. The British Embassy in Abidjan can register the death and advise on local funeral directors. They cannot pay for or arrange repatriation.
destinations.seo_title: Ivory Coast to UK Repatriation: Guidance for Families
destinations.seo_description: Someone has died in Ivory Coast. Repatriation to the UK takes 2-4 weeks. Etat civil, French translation, and export permits all handled. Contact us 24/7.
